content.full_text:
78-05-116

May 18, 1978


Dean Lan
Asian State Employees Association
Post Office Box 22361
Sacramento, California 95822

Dear Mr. Lan:

This letter confirms the informal advice that I gave you on May 10,
1978, in response to questions presented by Norah Quimby of the
Asian State Employees Association ("ASEA"). Specifically,
Ms. Quimby inquired whether a registered lobbyist may purchase
tickets to ASEA's Third Installation Dinner-Dance which will be
held on June 3, 1978 and, if so, whether a lobbyist must report
the purchase of the tickets on the lobbyist's disclosure report.

My understanding of the facts regarding the operation of ASEA and
the Third Installation Dinner-Dance is as follows:

- ASEA is an ongoing non-profit organization formed in 1975 to
represent ASIAN people who are employed in state service.

- Many of ASEA's members are agency officials as defined in
Government Code Section 82004l/ (i.e. they are members,
officers, employees or consultants of state agencies and
participate in administrative action in other than a purely
clerical, secretarial or ministerial capacity).

- ASEA's primary source of funds, other than the annual dues
of $12, is through fundraising events such as the Dinner-
Dance.

- ASEA has never made campaign contributions to state candidates,
elected state officers or their supporting committees and
does not anticipate making any such contributions in the
future.

- The tickets to the Dinner-Dance will be sold for $10 and
the surplus, if any, will be used to further the goals of
the organization as a whole and will not be used to make a
gift to any ASEA member.


_1/ All further references are to the Government Code.

Mr. Dean Lan                           -2-                           May 18, 1978


With regard to the issue of whether a lobbyist would be prohibited
from purchasing tickets to the Dinner-Dance, the following Sections
might be applicable:

     86202 - This Section prohibits a lobbyist from making,
     arranging for, or acting as an agent or interme-
     diary in the making of, a campaign contribution
     to an elected state officer, a state candidate
     or a committee supporting a state candidate.

     86203 - This Section prohibits a lobbyist from making,
     arranging for, or acting as an agent or interme-
     diary in the making of, gifts aggregating more
     than $10 in a calendar month to a state candi-
     date, an elected state officer, a legislative
     official or an agency official.

Since you have stated that ASEA has never made campaign contributions,
does not anticipate making contributions in the future, and will not
use any of the surplus from the Dinner-Dance to make a gift to any
member, the Commission staff has determined that if a registered
lobbyist purchases tickets to the Dinner-Dance, he or she will not
have participated in any of the activities prohibited by Sections
86202 or 86203. Accordingly, a registered lobbyist may purchase
tickets to the Dinner-Dance.

     In addition, a lobbyist will not have to report the purchase of
tickets to the Dinner-Dance on the lobbyist disclosure report, unless
the tickets are later given to an elected state official, legislative
official, agency official or the immediate family of any such official.
The gift of tickets to any official described above would constitute
an "activity expense" as defined in the Fair Political Practices
Commission's lobbyist accounting and reporting regulation, 2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 18615, and must be reported on the lobbyist's report.
Such a gift would be subject to the limitation on gifts provided in
Government Code Section 86203.

If I can be of further assistance, do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,


Alan Herndon
Compliance Representative

AH:bw

Mar 10, 1978.

To Whom It May Concern:

Asian State Employees Association is having its third Installation
Dinner Dance. It will be held at Wong's Islander, 5675 Freeport
Blvd. on Saturday, June 3, 1978. At 6pm. there will No Host
cocktails. At 7 pm there will be dinner and at 9 pm. dancing.
The proceeds of the dinner will be used primarily to defray
the cost of the installation dinner.

The Asian State Employees Association came about in the Spring
of 1975. As a non-profit group, the purpose of A.S.E.A. is
to provide for the needs and concerns of Asians employed in
State Service. The goals of the organization involve advising
and working with state government in areas of concern to the
membership:

1. Promoting career opportunities among the members
   and other qualified Asians.

2. Preparing members to become effective in job
   interviews and promotions.

3. Providing advocacy service to the Asian community
   to insure their legal rights to services provided
   by the state.

4. Participating in the development of state policies
   and programs that meet the needs of the Asian
   Community.

                                        Sincerely
                                        [signature]
                                        Dean Lan, President

ASIAN STATE EMPLOYEES ASSOCIATION
P.O. BOX 22361, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95822

ASIAN STATE
EMPLOYEES ASSOCIATION

Cordially Invites you
to Attend the

THIRD ANNUAL
INSTALLATION DINNER DANCE

SPECIAL GUEST SPEAKER
CONGRESSMAN NORMAN MINETA

KAITY TONG, KCRA ANCHORWOMAN,
MISTRESS OF CEREMONY

SATURDAY, JUNE 3, 1978

WONG'S ISLANDER
567S Freeport Blvd.

6 p.m. NO HOST COCKTAILS
7 p.m. DINNER
9 p.m. DANCE

$10.00 Per person

For Ticket Information Call 421-4442
